LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ON PLAINTIFF'S GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND PRODUCTS LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND PRODUCTS LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. THE GOVERNMENT CODE DOES NOT AUTHORIZE A GENERAL NEGLIGENCE OR PRODUCTS LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY AND, PURSUANT TO THE CALIFORNIA TORT CLAIMS ACT, A PUBLIC ENTITY CANNOT BE LIABLE TO ANOTHER ABSENT AN EXPRESS STATUTORY BASIS OF LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff'S General Negligence And Products Liability Causes Of Action
Off Calendar Sep-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ON PLAINTIFF'S GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND PRODUCTS LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION. Appearances: Bryan Lamb (421.2800) for plaintiff and responding party; David A. Delbon of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3962) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted without leave to amend as to general negligence and products liability causes of action. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. The Government Code does not authorize a general negligence or products liability cause of action against a public entity and, pursuant to the California Tort Claims Act, a public entity cannot be liable to another absent an express statutory basis of liability. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
